personSummary.value: Former goldfish keeper who calms when she can see the aquarium and prefers slow counting before standing or transfers. Post-lunch hallway walks may tire her, especially if the route continues past the aquarium bench. She follows cues best when staff count slowly from one to three and keep the aquarium in view during midday care.

value.description: Post-lunch walks have recently required rest near the aquarium bench.

citations.quote: asked to sit near the aquarium

value.description: Aquarium view and slow counting supported midday medication acceptance.

value: Pause near the aquarium bench after lunch before returning to the room.
Count slowly from one to three before standing or transfer cues.
Position the chair so she can see the aquarium during midday care.

citations.quote: moved the chair so she could see the aquarium
counted slowly from one to three

value: Continuing the post-lunch walk past the aquarium bench without a rest.
Standing or transfer help without a slow count.

citations.quote: walk continued past the aquarium bench
prefers people to count before helping her stand

value.approach: Plan a short rest at the aquarium bench and shorten the route if she asks to sit.
Keep the aquarium in view and count slowly before asking her to stand.

citations.quote: accepted after staff moved the chair
calms when she can watch fish move slowly

handoffBrief.value: Setsuko Ito calms near the aquarium and needs slow counting before standing; shorten post-lunch walks if she asks to rest near the fish.

value.claim: Watch post-lunch fatigue if the hallway route passes the aquarium bench without a pause.

citations.quote: stopped and said she needed to rest
became tired during the hallway walk
